next_steps: Perform transesophageal echocardiography (TEE) immediately to rule out infective endocarditis and aortic valvular/root involvement
Obtain emergent CT angiography of the chest and abdomen with focus on the thoracic aorta to evaluate for mycotic aneurysm expansion or acute aortic pathology
Continue targeted antibiotic therapy for methicillin-sensitive Staphylococcus aureus (MSSA) based on sensitivity, ensuring adequate coverage for potential deep-seated infection
Maintain strict blood pressure control with intravenous agents to manage hypertension and minimize stress on the aortic wall
Consult infectious disease for management of persistent bacteremia and evaluation of duration of therapy
Consult cardiothoracic surgery for evaluation of suspected aortic mycotic aneurysm or structural valvular involvement
